title: Corning Expands AI Data Center Connectivity Portfolio with PRIZM® TMT Technology
description: CORNING, N.Y.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Corning Incorporated (NYSE: GLW) today announced a collaboration with US Conec to become a licensee of PRIZM® TMT optical ferrule technology, a solution that allows for higher fiber counts in tighter spaces inside data centers. The addition of PRIZM® TMT expands Corning’s full spectrum of high‐density optical connectivity solutions for next‐generation AI networks. “AI infrastructure is pushing optical connectivity into new and more demanding environments,” said M

title: HIMSS26: Imprivata Introduces Agentic Identity Management to Secure and Govern AI Agents
description: What You Should Know The Launch: At HIMSS26, Imprivata, a global leader in healthcare access management, announced the launch of Agentic Identity Management, a new platform capability designed specifically to secure and govern AI agents across healthcare environments. The Core Problem: As health systems deploy agentic AI to autonomously handle clinical documentation, prior authorizations, and ... Read More

title: From Hype To Outcomes: How VCs Recalibrate Around Agentic AI
description: Investors are prioritizing startups that demonstrate real production usage, measurable outcomes and revenue — focusing less on flashy demos and more on proven business impact, writes guest author Harsha Kapre, head of Snowflake Ventures.

title: Scintil Releases DWDM Laser Source Evaluation Kit for Scale-Up AI Networks
description: LOS ANGELES, March 11, 2026 — As AI systems scale from single-rack scale up network systems to multi-rack configurations up to 1,000s of AI processors, copper interconnects have reached their limits in speed, density, and reach. Future scale-up networks require an optical architecture that delivers higher bandwidth density at lower power with maximum processor utilization. [...]

title: Zymtrace raises $12.2M to optimize AI workload performance across GPU infrastructure
description: Artificial intelligence infrastructure optimization platform startup Zymtrace announced today that it has raised $12.2 million in funding, including a newly closed $8.5 million seed round, to develop its platform, expand enterprise deployments and grow its U.S. go-to-market team. Founded in 2024, Zymtrace offers a platform designed to analyze and optimize the performance of AI workloads [...] The post Zymtrace raises $12.2M to optimize AI workload performance across GPU infrastructure appeared first on SiliconANGLE .
